title: Welcome to Rust! 🦀
description: What is Rust and why is everyone talking about it?

slides.title: Meet Ferris! 🦀
slides.content:
# What is Rust?

Rust is a modern programming language designed for **performance**, **safety**, and **concurrency**.

It was originally created at Mozilla but has since taken the programming world by storm!

slides.title: The Sweet Spot 🎯
slides.content:
Most languages force you to choose between two things:

1. **High Level:** Languages like Python or JavaScript. Easy to write, but slower.
2. **Low Level:** Languages like C or C++. Extremely fast, but you manage memory manually (which leads to lots of bugs!).

**Rust says: Why not both?** It gives you low-level control with high-level ease!

quizData.question: What two things does Rust aim to balance?
quizData.explanation: Rust aims to provide the performance of low-level languages (like C) with the safety and ergonomics of high-level languages.

slides.title: Safety First 🛡️
slides.content:
# No More Crashes!

The biggest feature of Rust is its **memory safety guarantee**.

In C++, it's easy to accidentally access memory that has been deleted, causing your app to crash instantly. Rust uses a unique system called *Ownership* (which we will learn later) to ensure this literally **cannot happen** at compile time!

quizData.question: What major problem does Rust prevent at compile time?

options.text: Memory safety bugs and random crashes

quizData.explanation: Rust prevents memory-related bugs, like accessing deleted memory, which are the #1 cause of crashes in languages like C/C++.

slides.title: Who uses Rust? 🏢
slides.content:
Everyone wants in on the crab!

* **Microsoft:** Rewriting core Windows components.
* **Linux:** Rust is the *only* language other than C allowed in the Linux Kernel.
* **Amazon, Google, Discord, Cloudflare:** All use Rust to power their blazing-fast backend services.
